incompatibleRuntime.title: This system is not supported by the bundled backend
incompatibleRuntime.description: BiWork opened, but the local BiWork backend backend cannot run on this Linux version. Please upgrade to a supported Linux distribution and restart BiWork.

incompleteInstallation.title: BiWork installation is incomplete
incompleteInstallation.description: This installation is missing required local resources, so BiWork backend cannot start. Please download and reinstall the latest BiWork. If the issue returns after reinstalling, check whether security or antivirus software quarantined BiWork backend.
incompleteInstallation.runtimeComponentDescription: This installation is missing required bundled runtime components, so {{resource}} cannot start. Reinstall the latest BiWork package. If it still happens after reinstalling, check whether security or antivirus software quarantined BiWork components.

packageArchitectureMismatch.title: BiWork package architecture mismatch
packageArchitectureMismatch.description: This BiWork package is for {{packageArch}}, but this Mac is {{deviceArch}}. You may have downloaded the wrong package. Please download and install the {{expectedArch}} package.

dataMigration.title: Local data migration failed
dataMigration.description: BiWork opened, but BiWork backend stopped while initializing local data. Reinstalling BiWork may not fix this. Send a diagnostics report or contact support so the local database migration can be inspected.

localDataRepair.title: Local data repair failed
localDataRepair.description: BiWork opened, but BiWork backend stopped while repairing local agent cache data. Reinstalling BiWork may not fix this. Send a diagnostics report or contact support so the local database can be inspected.

startupDirectory.title: Startup directory is unavailable
startupDirectory.description: BiWork opened, but BiWork backend could not start because the configured work or log directory is missing, unavailable, or blocked by permissions. Check that the configured directories exist and are writable, then restart BiWork.

recoverableDatabaseCorruption.title: Local data is corrupted
recoverableDatabaseCorruption.description: BiWork detected that the local database is corrupted and cannot continue startup. After confirmation, BiWork will back up the old database and create a new local database to continue startup. Historical local data will not be shown for now, and the old database will be kept as a backup.
recoverableDatabaseCorruption.confirmRebuild: Back up old DB and rebuild new DB
recoverableDatabaseCorruption.diagnosticsHint: Sending diagnostics will not modify the local database.
recoverableDatabaseCorruption.rebuildFailed: Failed to back up old DB and rebuild new DB
